verses.commentary: Sarah's age. 127 years. She is the only woman in Scripture whose age at death is recorded, showing her importance.
Sarah dies at Hebron. In the land of Canaan, still as foreigners. Abraham mourns and weeps. Grief is expressed. The Hebrew allows for formal mourning rituals.
Abraham rises from mourning. Spoke to the Hittites. The local inhabitants. He needs to negotiate for a burial place.
Abraham identifies as foreigner and stranger, resident alien. Sell me property. He wants legal ownership. Bury my dead. Burial in the promised land stakes a claim for the future.
Respectful response. Mighty prince (literally a prince of God). They recognize Abraham's status. Choicest tombs offered. None will refuse. Generous but not what Abraham wants.
Abraham stands and bows. Respect for the local inhabitants. He's negotiating carefully.
If you are willing. Listen to me. Intercede with Ephron. Specific request. Ephron owns the field Abraham wants.
Cave of Machpelah. Ask him to sell. For the full price (literally for full silver). Abraham wants legal purchase, not a gift. Among you. Permanent presence in the land.
Public setting. The gate is where business is conducted. In the hearing of all. Legal witnesses present.
Ephron offers it as a gift. I give you. The presence of my people. Public generosity. But this creates obligation and isn't legal ownership in the same way.
Abraham bows again. Respectful but persistent.
Abraham insists. I will pay the price. Accept it from me. He wants purchase, not gift. So I can bury my dead there. Purpose stated.
Ephron names a price. Four hundred shekels. A large sum. But what is that between you and me? Minimizing while actually demanding payment. Bury your dead, pretending generosity while expecting money.

Abraham agrees. Weighed out, payment in silver by weight. In the hearing of the Hittites. Public transaction. According to merchant weight. Legal and official. Abraham pays the inflated price without haggling.
Deeded, established, confirmed legally. Field, cave, trees. Everything included. The borders defined.
To Abraham as his property. In the presence of all. Legal witnesses. The transaction is complete and binding.
Abraham buries Sarah. In the cave. In the land of Canaan. Sarah rests in the promised land. This is the first piece of Canaan legally owned by Abraham.

Deeded to Abraham. By the Hittites. As a burial site. The transaction concludes. This cave becomes the patriarchs' burial place. Abraham, Sarah, Isaac, Rebekah, Jacob, and Leah will all be buried here (49:29-32). A foothold in the promised land.
